description: Trigger discrimination: PR-time context write-back enforcement vs session-start context reading (agent-startup-context-gate), pipeline design/editing (ci-pipeline-architect), and the cadenced chat sweep (chat-backlog-reconciliation).

cases.prompt: PRs keep changing the schema and API without anyone updating the repo context map — make CI fail PRs that touch important paths without a matching context update.
cases.reason: Enforcing context WRITE-BACK on PRs (paths list, co-update rule, protocol, gate spec) is this skill; it is not session-start reading, and the whole-pipeline design authority is not what was asked — one policy gate is.

cases.prompt: You're resuming in a repo after two weeks — verify the repo identity and read the context map and status docs before touching code.
cases.reason: Reading and verifying context at task start is the startup gate; no PR-time enforcement is being designed.

cases.prompt: Design our CI/CD pipeline — stages for lint, build, tests, security, artifact, deploy gates, and which checks block merge.
cases.reason: The delivery pipeline's stage graph and merge-blocking semantics are ci-pipeline-architect; the context gate is one policy check that would be wired INTO such a pipeline.

cases.prompt: Extract the decisions from last month's AI chats into dated repo docs and audit which ones actually happened.
cases.reason: Draining ephemeral chat into audited repo docs on a cadence is the reconciliation sweep; the CI gate acts at PR time on code-path changes, not on conversations.

cases.prompt: Here's the approved context-gate spec — now edit .github/workflows/ to add the job and make it a required check.
cases.reason: Editing pipeline definitions and required-check config is ci-pipeline-architect's manual-only territory; the context gate skill produces the spec and stops at the handoff.
